facts: Bridgestone Corporation is a Japanese multinational tire and rubber manufacturing company based in Kyōbashi, Tokyo.
The company was founded on 1 March 1931 by Shojiro Ishibashi in Kurume, Fukuoka, Japan.
Key people include Yasuhiro Morita (Global CEO).
Products include tires, golf equipment, and automotive components.
Bridgestone supplies automakers with vibration-isolating components, such as engine mounts.
Bridgestone also supplies air springs for trucks, automobiles and train carriages.
Bridgestone markets aluminium wheels and other automotive accessories.
As of July 2018, the Bridgestone Group has 181 production facilities in 24 countries.
The company opened its first postwar overseas plant in Singapore in 1965 and commenced production in Thailand in 1969.
Bridgestone Americas was established in 1967 to serve as the U.S. sales representative branch.

In 1983, the company purchased a plant in Tennessee from Firestone Tire and Rubber Company for its first North American manufacturing facility and started production of radial tires for trucks and buses.
Bridgestone operates a steel cord plant in Clarksville, Tennessee, named Bridgestone Metalpha, which is ranked as the top provider in the global steel cord market.
In April 2012, Bridgestone Americas opened a new $100 million Bridgestone Americas Technical Center in Akron that houses 450 employees.
In June 2022, Bridgestone opened a $21 million Advanced Tire Production Center in Akron that produces racing tires for the NTT IndyCar Series and all Firestone Firehawk racing tires.
Bridgestone opened a $6 million passenger tire test track adjacent to the Akron facility in Autumn 2022.
Bridgestone has invested more than $125 million in its Akron operations since 2012.

Nashville-based Bridgestone Americas, Inc. develops, manufactures and markets Bridgestone, Firestone, and associate brand tires for consumers, automotive and commercial vehicle original equipment manufacturers, and those in the agricultural, forestry and mining industries.
The company also produces air springs, roofing materials, synthetic rubber and industrial fibers and textiles.
In 2017, Bridgestone Americas consolidated many business units into a single building in downtown Nashville where nearly 2,000 employees work.

In April 2020, Bridgestone Americas announced plans to restart its North American commercial tire plants and Firestone Industrial Products and Firestone Building Products manufacturing facilities due to demand during the COVID-19 pandemic.

Bridgestone Aircraft Tire moved its Miami, Florida aviation operation to a new 160,000-square-foot facility in Mayodan, North Carolina, expected to be completed by mid-2007, creating approximately 95 new jobs.

Diversified operations encompass automotive components, industrial products, polyurethane foam products, construction materials, parts and materials for electronic equipment, bicycles and sporting goods.
Bridgestone's diversified business generates about one-fourth of total sales in the Bridgestone Group.
Bridgestone was the sole tire supplier for Formula One from 1997 to 2010.
Firestone has been the single tire provider for the IndyCar Series and its feeder series Indy NXT since 2000.
From April 2023, Bridgestone became the main tire supplier for the Super Taikyu championship series.
Bridgestone was the exclusive tire supplier for MotoGP from 2009 to 2015.
Bridgestone was the Official Tire of the Olympic Games until the end of 2024.

In January 2026, Bridgestone agreed to sell its tyre reinforcement business in China and Thailand to Bekaert for €60 million as part of a strategy to enhance competitiveness, including a long-term tyre cord supply agreement.
Bridgestone EU distributes more than 25 million tires a year through 17 national sales subsidiaries and 2 distributors, directly employing over 12,000 people with Mr. Tsuda as CEO.
The plant at Ulyanovsk, Russia and the marketing office in Moscow were sold in December 2023 as Bridgestone withdrew from the Russian market following the Russian invasion of Ukraine.
Total employees numbered 129,262 in 2023.
Revenue was ¥4.11 trillion (US$37.45 billion) for the fiscal year ended 31 December 2023.
Founder Shojiro Ishibashi died on 11 September 1976.
